steps.title: Recognize the perpendicularity property
steps.content:
At any point on a circle, the tangent line is perpendicular to the radius. The radius connects the center $(2, 7)$ to the tangency point $(a, -4)$, so its slope is:
$$m_{\text{radius}} = \frac{-4 - 7}{a - 2} = \frac{-11}{a - 2}$$
For two perpendicular lines, their slopes multiply to $-1$.

steps.title: Solve using the perpendicularity condition
steps.content:
Set up the equation:
$$\frac{-11}{a - 2} \cdot \frac{6}{5} = -1$$
Simplify the left side:
$$\frac{-66}{5(a - 2)} = -1$$
Multiply both sides by $5(a - 2)$:
$$-66 = -5(a - 2)$$
Expand and solve:
$$-66 = -5a + 10$$
$$-76 = -5a$$
$$a = \frac{76}{5}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
